sections.title: Biological Overview
content: Bulbasaur is a symbiotic species featuring a nutrient-rich bulb on its back. This bulb is planted at birth and grows in tandem with the Pokémon.
It can survive for days without food, relying solely on solar energy stored within its bulb through photosynthesis.

sections.title: Design Origins
content: Inspired by the Dicynodont, an extinct synapsid, combined with characteristics of common frogs.
Its name is a portmanteau of the English words 'Bulb' and the Greek 'Saurus' (Lizard).

sections.title: GBL Tactical History
content: A dominant contender in the Little Cup format.
The combination of Vine Whip's high energy generation and the legacy move Frenzy Plant makes it one of the most efficient Grass-type attackers in the meta.

sections.title: Terminal Data: Anomalies
content: Bulbasaur is the only dual-type starter in the Kanto region.
It holds the first index entry (#001) in the National Pokédex.

sections.title: Maturation Profile
content: As the bulb on its back matures into a bud, Ivysaur's weight increases, forcing it to develop stronger leg muscles.
When the bud is close to blooming, it emits a distinctively sweet aroma and the Pokémon begins spending significantly more time basking in direct sunlight.

sections.title: Historical Media Logs
content: Famously documented in the 'Bulbasaur's Mysterious Garden' logs, which show groups of the species gathering to evolve synchronously under moonlight.

sections.title: Apex Flora
content: The bulb has fully flowered into a massive bloom capable of absorbing large amounts of solar energy to power devastating beam attacks.
The scent from its flower is known to calm human emotions and can attract other Pokémon from miles away.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A premiere choice for the Ultra League. Its Shadow variant is particularly feared for its ability to overwhelm Water-types with raw Frenzy Plant pressure.

sections.title: Thermal Core
content: Charmander's health is tied directly to the flame on its tail. If the flame flickers weakly, its health is declining; if it goes out, the Pokémon's life ends.
Steam is known to rise from the tip of its tail if it is caught in the rain.

sections.title: Mythological Baseline
content: Derived from the 'Fire Salamander' of European myth, which was believed to be capable of surviving inside active furnaces.

sections.title: Aggression Surge
content: Evolution triggers a surge in adrenaline and aggression. Charmeleon is highly territorial and will seek out opponents far larger than itself.
In battle, it exhales bluish-white flames, raising local temperatures to hazardous levels.

sections.title: System Designation: FLAME POKEMON
content: Charizard represents the final biological maturation of the Kanto Fire-type lineage. It is characterized by its powerful wings and a thermal core that powers a perpetual flame at the tip of its tail.
Despite its destructive potential, Charizard adheres to a primitive code of honor: it will never direct its full heat toward an opponent it perceives as significantly weaker than itself.

sections.title: Morphological Inspiration
content: While its English designation suggests a 'Charred Lizard,' its physiology is more closely aligned with the European 'Western Dragon'—a winged, fire-breathing reptilian apex predator.
Its Japanese name, 'Lizardon,' highlights its lizard-like ancestry over its draconic appearance.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: In the Go Battle League (GBL), Charizard has evolved from a niche pick to a premier meta threat. Its utility shifted dramatically following the 'Wing Attack' energy-generation buff and the introduction of the legacy move 'Blast Burn'.

sections.title: Terminal Data: Anomalies
content: STAT CLONE: Charizard shares the exact base stat distribution with the Johto starter, Typhlosion.
DUAL EVOLUTION: It is one of only two known species capable of two distinct Mega Evolutions (X and Y).

sections.title: Hydraulic Defense
content: Squirtle's shell is not just for protection; its rounded shape and surface grooves help minimize resistance in water, enabling the Pokémon to swim at high speeds.
When threatened, it withdraws into its shell and sprays water from its mouth with high pressure.

sections.title: Maturation and Longevity
content: The long, furry tail is a symbol of longevity. As it ages, scratches may appear on its shell as evidence of its toughness as a battler.
It is popular as a pet because its tail is considered to bring good luck.

sections.title: Hydraulic Artillery
content: Blastoise features pressurized water cannons inside its shell. These cannons can punch through thick steel and are accurate enough to hit small targets from over 160 feet away.
It deliberately makes itself heavy to withstand the recoil of the water jets it fires.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Functions as a bulky Water-type closer in the Great League. Hydro Cannon is its primary win condition.

sections.title: Defense Mechanisms
content: Caterpie releases a powerful, stench-filled odor from its bright red antenna when it feels threatened. This serves to repel bird Pokémon predators.
Its feet are tipped with suction pads that allow it to scale vertical surfaces like trees.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This POKéMON is vulnerable to attack while its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp venomous stinger on its head.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Almost incapable of moving, this POKéMON can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Flies at high speed and attacks using its large venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Very protective of its sprawling territorial area, this POKéMON will fiercely peck at any intruder.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as MAGIKARP.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Living wherever there is food available, it ceaselessly scav­ enges for edibles the entire day.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It uses its whis­ kers to maintain its balance. It apparently slows down if they are cut off.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speed to stay airborne.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Moves silently and stealthily. Eats the eggs of birds, such as PIDGEY and SPEAROW, whole.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is rumored that the ferocious warning markings on its belly differ from area to area.

sections.title: Electrical Capacitance
content: Pikachu stores electricity in the red pouches on its cheeks. When it releases this energy, it can cause small lightning bolts to strike.
It raises its tail to check its surroundings; it is occasionally struck by lightning in this position.

sections.title: Global Iconography
content: Pikachu was not originally intended to be the series mascot; the role was initially planned for Clefairy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high voltage power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Burrows deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Curls up into a spiny ball when threatened. It can roll while curled up to attack or escape.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Although small, its venomous barbs render this POKéMON dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The female's horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger its horns, the more powerful its secreted venom.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: An aggressive POKéMON that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break the prey's bones.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A timid fairy POKéMON that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. The tail splits from its tip as it grows older.

sections.title: Mystical Longevity
content: Ninetales is said to live for a thousand years. Each of its nine tails is imbued with a different supernatural power.
It is highly intelligent and understands human speech. It is also notoriously vengeful; grabbing one of its tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

sections.title: Mythological Baseline
content: Based on the Kitsune of Japanese folklore—many-tailed foxes with shapeshifting and fire-breathing abilities.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has no eyes. Instead, it relies on its ultrasonic cries for echolocation to flit about in darkness.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Once it strikes, it will not stop draining energy from the victim even if it gets too heavy to fly.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: During the day, it keeps its face buried in the ground. At night, it wanders around sowing its seeds.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The fluid that oozes from its mouth isn't drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by draw­ ing nutrients from the bug host.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It stays mostly in dark, damp places, the preference not of the bug, but of the big mush­ rooms on its back.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.

sections.title: Subterranean Life
content: Diglett lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It only comes to the surface when it is not sunny.
Farmers often keep Diglett because it tills the earth while burrowing, leaving the soil perfectly conditioned for planting crops.

sections.title: Coordinated Subterranean Assault
content: Dugtrio consists of three heads that move in perfect synchronization. They can burrow through even the hardest soil to depths of 60 miles.
By burrowing together, they create massive seismic disturbances that can be detected from several miles away.

sections.title: Nocturnal Obsessions
content: Meowth is fascinated by round, shiny objects. It spends its nights wandering city streets looking for dropped loose change.
The gold coin on its forehead can glow, and the Pokémon is often used as a symbol of prosperity and good fortune.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

sections.title: Psychic Threshold
content: Psyduck is constantly plagued by a severe headache. When the pain reaches a critical peak, it unknowingly releases powerful psychic energy.
Because these powers are only activated in a state of extreme stress, Psyduck usually has no memory of using them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Often seen swim­ ming elegantly by lake shores. It is often mistaken for the Japanese monster, Kappa.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment then thrashing away the next instant.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Always furious and tenacious to boot. It will not abandon chasing its quarry until it is caught.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A POKéMON that has been admired since the past for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming than trying to stand.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breast stroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

sections.title: Preemptive Teleportation
content: Abra sleeps for 18 hours a day. Even while asleep, it can sense incoming threats and will reflexively teleport to safety.
If it doesn't get enough sleep, it loses its ability to utilize its psychic powers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches just by being close by.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its brain can out­ perform a super­ computer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5,000.

sections.title: Physical Discipline
content: Machop trains by lifting boulders and practicing various forms of martial arts. Its muscles never get sore, no matter how much it exercises.
A single Machop is strong enough to hurl 100 adult humans simultaneously.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It happily carries heavy cargo to toughen up. It willingly does hard work for people.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Using its heavy muscles, it throws powerful punches that can send the victim clear over the horizon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A carnivorous POKéMON that traps and eats bugs. It uses its root feet to soak up needed moisture.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A Pokémon that appears to be a plant. It captures unwary prey by dousing them with a toxic powder.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by its stinging acid.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The tentacles are normally kept short. On hunts, they are extended to ensnare and immobilize prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Found in fields and mountains. Mistaking them for boulders, people often step or trip on them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Rolls down slopes to move. It rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing its direction.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its boulder-like body is extremely hard. It can easily withstand dynamite blasts without damage.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything completely flat in little time.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Very competitive, this POKéMON will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is always so absent-minded that it won't react, even if its flavorful tail is bitten.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The SHELLDER that is latched onto SLOWPOKE's tail is said to feed on the host's left over scraps.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses THUNDER WAVE and similar moves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Formed by several MAGNEMITEs linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. It is used much like a metal sword.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The protruding horn on its head is very hard. It is used for bashing through thick ice.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Appears in filthy areas. Thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When attacked, it launches its horns in quick volleys. Its innards have never been seen.

sections.title: Gaseous Composition
content: Gastly's body is 95% gas. It can slip through any crack or crevice to reach its prey.
The gas is highly toxic; if it envelops a human, they will lose consciousness instantly.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from an­ other dimension.

sections.title: Shadow Mimicry
content: Gengar hides in the shadows of people or objects at night and absorbs the heat from their bodies, causing a sudden, chilling drop in temperature.
It is said that if you feel a sudden cold shiver, a Gengar is hiding right behind you.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A classic 'Glass Cannon' in the Ultra League. Shadow Claw and Shadow Ball combined with its high attack stat make it a lethal closer if shields are down.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It burrows through the ground at a speed of 50 mph while feeding on large boulders.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Puts enemies to sleep then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of PSI moves such as HYPNOSIS and CONFUSION.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its pincers are not only powerful weapons, they are used for balance when walking sideways.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The large pincer has 10000 hp of crushing power. However, its huge size makes it unwieldy to use.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a POKé BALL, they have zapped many people.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It stores electric energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Using telepathy only they can receive, they always form a cluster of six EXEGGCUTE.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an EXEGGCUTE.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Because it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this POKéMON's real face.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to KO targets.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a tingling sensation when it licks enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Because it stores several kinds of toxic gases in its body, it is prone to exploding without warning.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Where two kinds of poison gases meet, 2 KOFFINGs can fuse into a WEEZING over many years.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its massive bones are 1000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A rare and elusive POKéMON that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to get it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to sea­ weed. Its vines shake as it walks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The infant rarely ventures out of its mother's protective pouch until it is 3 years old.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Known to shoot down flying bugs with precision blasts of ink from the surface of the water.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Capable of swim­ ming backwards by rapidly flapping its wing-like pectoral fins and stout tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname of the Water Queen.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: An enigmatic POKéMON that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If interrupted while it is miming, it will slap around the offender with its broad hands.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It seductively wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Normally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing it around and toss it hard.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

sections.title: Nautical Intelligence
content: Lapras is highly intelligent and capable of understanding human speech. It is exceptionally gentle and enjoys ferrying people across the ocean on its back.
Due to its peaceful nature, it has been overhunted by humans, bringing it to the brink of extinction.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it spots an enemy, its body transfigures into an almost-perfect copy of its opponent.

sections.title: Genetic Mutability
content: Eevee possesses an unstable genetic code that allows it to evolve into various forms depending on its environment.
Its radiation-sensitive DNA can mutate rapidly when exposed to certain evolutionary stones.

sections.title: Terminal Data: Anomalies
content: Eevee holds the record for the most possible branched evolutions of any species in the National Pokédex.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin which is often mistaken for a mermaid's.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It accumulates negative ions in the atmosphere to blast out 10000- volt lightning bolts.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When storing thermal energy in its body, its temperature could soar to over 1600 degrees.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A POKéMON that consists entirely of programming code. Capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically resurrected from fossils.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A prehistoric POKéMON that died out when its heavy shell made it impossible to catch prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A POKéMON that was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A ferocious, pre­ historic POKéMON that goes for the enemy's throat with its serrated saw-like fangs.

sections.title: Metabolic Extremes
content: Snorlax spends its entire day sleeping and eating. It needs to consume 900 pounds of food daily to feel satisfied.
Its stomach is strong enough to digest even moldy or rotten food without any ill effects.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A legendary bird POKéMON that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A legendary bird POKéMON that is said to appear from clouds while dropping enormous lightning bolts.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Known as the legendary bird of fire. Every flap of its wings creates a dazzling flash of flames.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Long considered a mythical POKéMON until recently when a small colony was found living underwater.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A mystical POKéMON that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

sections.title: Oceanic Guardian
content: Despite its bulky appearance, Dragonite can fly faster than the speed of sound. It is a kind-hearted Pokémon that leads lost ships in storms back to safety.
In battle, it is nearly unstoppable, capable of unleashing massive elemental attacks.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A powerhouse in the Master League. Dragon Breath and Dragon Claw provide relentless pressure, while Superpower or Hurricane offers vital coverage.

sections.title: Artificially Engineered Psionics
content: Mewtwo was created by scientists through years of horrific gene-splicing experiments based on the DNA of Mew.
It lacks a sympathetic heart and is said to possess the most savage temperament among all Pokémon.

sections.title: The Genetic Origin
content: Mew is said to possess the genetic composition of all Pokémon. Because it can make itself invisible at will, it is rarely seen by humans.
Scientists believe Mew is the ancestor of all Pokémon species.

sections.title: Aromatic Regulation
content: Chikorita uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity of its surroundings. It loves to sunbathe.
It emits a sweet, refreshing aroma from its leaf that has the power to calm down battling Pokémon and create a friendly atmosphere.

sections.title: Botanical Design
content: Influenced by the long-necked Sauropod dinosaurs (specifically the Brachiosaurus), blended with a pear and an unfurling leaf.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The scent of spices comes from around its neck. Somehow, sniffing it makes you want to fight.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The aroma that rises from its petals contains a substance that calms aggressive feelings.

sections.title: Combustion Vents
content: Cyndaquil is usually timid and curls into a ball when startled. It has several fire-emitting vents on its back.
If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back. The intensity of these flames indicates its level of exhaustion.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Be careful if it turns its back during battle. It means that it will attack with the fire on its back.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If its rage peaks, it becomes so hot that anything that touches it will instantly go up in flames.

sections.title: Mastication Reflex
content: Totodile has a playful but dangerous habit of biting anything it sees moving. Its jaws are powerful enough to crush heavy objects.
Even if its trainer is just trying to show affection, Totodile may accidentally cause injury with its powerful snap.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a very nervous nature. It stands up high on its tail so it can scan wide areas.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other POKéMON to enter.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is very timid. It will be afraid to move if it is alone. But it will be active if it is in a group.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It is hard to tell which end is which.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Having four wings allows it to fly more quickly and quietly so it can sneak up on prey without its noticing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It shoots positive and negative elec­ tricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea's surface from a depth of over three miles.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is not yet skilled at storing electricity. It may send out a jolt if amused or startled.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a very soft body. If it starts to roll, it will bounce all over and be impossible to stop.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To share its happiness, it flies around the world seeking kind- hearted people.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Because its wings aren't yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always star­ ing at something.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If static elec­ tricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won't grow.

sections.title: Luminescent Guidance
content: The tip of Ampharos's tail glows so brightly that it can be seen from space. In ancient times, people used its light as a beacon for ships.
It was once heavily utilized in lighthouses along the Johto coast before the advent of industrial lighting.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A versatile Electric-type in the Ultra League. Its access to the fast-charging Volt Switch and the coverage move Brutal Swing or Focus Blast makes it a dangerous 'safe swap'.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: BELLOSSOM gather at times and appear to dance. They say that the dance is a ritual to summon the sun.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The tip of its tail, which con­ tains oil that is lighter than wa­ ter, lets it swim without drowning.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: By keeping still and listening in­ tently, it can tell what is in even wild, fast- moving rivers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Despite appearing to be a tree, its body is closer to rocks and stones. It is very weak to water.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If POLIWAG and POLIWHIRL hear its echoing cry, they respond by gather­ ing from far and wide.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To keep from being blown away by the wind, they gather in clusters. They do enjoy gentle breezes, though.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The bloom on top of its head opens and closes as the temperature fluc­ tuates up and down.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Blown by seasonal winds, it circles the globe, scattering cotton spores as it goes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It may drop out of the sky suddenly. If attacked by a SPEAROW, it will violently shake its leaves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It converts sun­ light into energy. In the darkness after sunset, it closes its petals and becomes still.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter win­ dows in the area.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This POKéMON lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This carefree POKéMON has an easy-going nature. While swimming, it always bumps into boat hulls.

sections.title: Psychic Sensitivity
content: Espeon is extremely loyal to any trainer it considers worthy. It is said to have developed its precognitive powers to protect its trainer from harm.
The fine fur covering its body is so sensitive that it can sense minute shifts in the air, allowing it to predict its opponent's next move and even the weather.

sections.title: Mythological Baseline
content: Inspired by the Bakeneko (monster cat) of Japanese folklore, specifically the Nekomata, which is often depicted with a forked tail.

sections.title: Nocturnal Synchronization
content: Umbreon evolved as a result of exposure to the moon's waves. It hides silently in darkness and waits for its foes to make a move.
The yellow rings on its body glow when it is excited or when it is exposed to moonlight, striking fear into the hearts of anyone nearby.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The definitive 'Tank' of the Great League. With astronomical defense and HP stats, Umbreon can outlast almost any opponent. Snarl provides elite energy generation for the community-day move Psychic or the heavy-hitting Foul Play.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has incredible intellect and in­ tuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tab­ lets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It hates light and shock. If attack­ ed, it inflates its body to pump up its counter­ strike.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent and bite.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside the armor is a total mystery.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When spotted, this POKéMON escapes backward by furi­ ously boring into the ground with its tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It flies straight at its target's face then clamps down on the star­ tled victim to inject poison.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its body has been compressed deep under the ground. As a result, it is even harder than a diamond.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Although it looks frightening, it is actually kind and affectionate. It is very popular among women.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is actually timid and easily spooked. If at­ tacked, it flails about to fend off its attacker.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

sections.title: Metallic Exoskeleton
content: Scizor has a body with the hardness of steel. It is not easily fazed by ordinary attacks. It flaps its wings to regulate its body temperature.
Its pincers contain steel and can smash any hard object into bits. It raises its pincers and waves them around to scare off predators.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The BERRIES it stores in its vase-like shell decompose and become a gooey liquid.

sections.title: Sap Obsession
content: Heracross is usually docile, but if it is disturbed while sipping honey or sap, it will use its powerful horn to hurl the intruder away.
It is capable of lifting objects more than 100 times its own weight with its impressive horn strength.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it sud­ denly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Although it is a good climber, it prefers to snap trees with its forelegs and eat fallen BERRIES.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn’t keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The shell on its back is just skin that has cooled and hardened. It breaks easily with a slight touch.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Although its legs are short, its rugged hooves prevent it from slipping, even on icy ground.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure for its beauty.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has superb ac­ curacy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It traps enemies with its suction- cupped tentacles then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: As it majestically swims, it doesn't care if REMORAID attach to it for scavenging its leftovers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its sturdy wings look heavy, but they are actually hollow and light, allowing it to fly freely in the sky.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is said that it usually hides in underwater caves. It can create whirlpools by yawning.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It swings its long snout around play­ fully, but because it is so strong, that can be dan­ gerous.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its TACKLE is strong enough to knock down a house.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This upgraded version of PORYGON is designed for space exploration. It can't fly, though.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where real­ ity is distorted.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A special fluid oozes from the tip of its tail. It paints the fluid everywhere to mark its territory.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is always bursting with en­ ergy. To make it­ self stronger, it keeps on fighting even if it loses.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If you become enchanted by its smooth, elegant, dance-like kicks, you may get drilled hard.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its lips are the most sensitive parts on its body. It always uses its lips first to examine things.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It rotates its arms to generate electricity, but it tires easily, so it charges up only a little bit.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Each and every time it inhales and exhales, hot embers dribble out of its mouth and nostrils.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its milk is packed with nutrition, making it the ultimate beverage for the sick or weary.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Anyone who takes even one bite of BLISSEY's egg be­ comes unfailingly caring and pleas­ ant to everyone.

sections.title: Electromagnetic Velocity
content: Raikou embodies the speed of lightning. The roars it emits send shock waves shuddering through the air and shake the ground as if lightning bolts had struck.
It is said to have descended with lightning. It can fire electrical blasts from the rain clouds on its back.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Volcanoes erupt when it barks. Un­ able to restrain its extreme power, it races headlong around the land.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Said to be the reincarnation of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It feeds on soil. After it has eaten a large mountain, it will fall asleep so it can grow.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its THRASHING can topple a mountain.

sections.title: Insurmountable Durability
content: Tyranitar is so overwhelmingly powerful, it can bring down a whole mountain to make its nest. It wanders about in mountains seeking new opponents to fight.
Its body is practically indestructible, so it is extremely difficult to inflict any significant damage upon it.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A niche but terrifying pick in the Master League. Its 'Smack Down' fast move can melt Flying-types (like Ho-Oh or Lugia) in seconds, though its many weaknesses require careful team composition.

sections.title: Guardian of the Seas
content: Lugia's wings pack devastating power—a light fluttering of them can blow apart regular houses. As a result, it chooses to live out of sight deep under the sea.
It is said to be the guardian of the seas and is capable of calming the most violent storms.

sections.title: Celestial Resurrection
content: Ho-Oh's feathers glow in seven colors depending on the angle at which they are struck by light. These feathers are said to bring happiness to the bearers.
Legend says this Pokémon flew over the burned tower in Ecruteak City and resurrected three deceased Pokémon as the Legendary Beasts.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A top-tier Master League titan. Incinerate and Brave Bird allow it to output massive damage, while Sacred Fire (a signature move) provides a high chance to debuff the opponent's attack.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This POKéMON wan­ ders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

sections.title: Arboreal Adaptation
content: Treecko has small hooks on the bottom of its feet that enable it to scale vertical walls. It is known for its cool and collected personality.
It makes its home in large trees and will fiercely defend its territory from anyone who approaches.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The leaves growing out of GROVYLE’s body are convenient for camouflaging it from enemies in the forest. This POKéMON is a master at climbing trees in jungles.

sections.title: Jungle Predator
content: Sceptile's agility is unmatched in the forest. The seeds on its back are filled with nutrients that can revitalize trees.
The leaves growing on its arms are as sharp as swords, allowing it to slice through thick branches and foes alike.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A high-pressure 'Glass Cannon' in the Great and Ultra Leagues. With Bullet Seed and Leaf Blade, it has one of the fastest charged move activation rates in the game.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: TORCHIC sticks with its TRAINER, following behind with unsteady steps. This POKéMON breathes fire of over 1,800 degrees F, including fireballs that leave the foe scorched black.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: COMBUSKEN toughens up its legs and thighs by running through fields and mountains. This POKéMON’s legs possess both speed and power, enabling it to dole out ten kicks in one second.

sections.title: Pyro-Kinetic Martial Arts
content: Blaziken has incredibly strong legs; it can easily clear a 30-story building in a single leap. Its punches can leave opponents scorched.
In battle, it blows out intense flames from its wrists, which intensify as it faces tougher opponents.

sections.title: Design Origins
content: Inspired by the Shamo breed of Japanese chicken, which was bred specifically for its fighting spirit and upright posture.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The fin on MUDKIP’s head acts as highly sensitive radar. Using this fin to sense movements of water and air, this POKéMON can determine what is taking place around it without using its eyes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The surface of MARSHTOMP’s body is enveloped by a thin, sticky film that enables it to live on land. This POKéMON plays in mud on beaches when the ocean tide is low.

sections.title: Hydro-Mechanical Prowess
content: Swampert is very strong and can easily drag a large boulder weighing more than a ton. It has powerful vision that enables it to see even in turbid water.
Its fins can sense subtle changes in the movement of air and water, allowing it to predict storms and incoming waves.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Widely considered the most dominant 'Mud Shot' user in GBL history. Its access to Hydro Cannon and Earthquake gives it near-perfect coverage across all leagues.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: At first sight, POOCHYENA takes a bite at anything that moves. This POKéMON chases after prey until the victim becomes exhausted. However, it may turn tail if the prey strikes back.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It chases down prey in a pack. It will never disobey the commands of a skilled Trainer.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: ZIGZAGOON restlessly wanders everywhere at all times. This POKéMON does so because it is very curious. It becomes interested in anything that it happens to see.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LINOONE always runs full speed and only in straight lines. If facing an obstacle, it makes a right-angle turn to evade it. This POKéMON is very challenged by gently curving roads.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Using the spikes on its rear end,   WURMPLE peels the bark off trees and feeds on the sap that oozes out. This POKéMON’s feet are tipped with suction pads that allow it to cling to glass without slipping.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SILCOON tethers itself to a tree branch using silk to keep from falling. There,  this POKéMON hangs quietly while it awaits evolution. It peers out of the silk cocoon through a small hole.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: BEAUTIFLY’s favorite food is the sweet pollen of flowers. If you want to see this POKéMON, just leave a potted flower by an open window. BEAUTIFLY is sure to come looking for pollen.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CASCOON makes its protective cocoon by wrapping its body entirely with a fine silk from its mouth. Once the silk goes around its body, it hardens. This POKéMON prepares for its evolution inside the cocoon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: DUSTOX is instinctively drawn to light. Swarms of this POKéMON are attracted by the bright lights of cities, where they wreak havoc by stripping the leaves off roadside trees for food.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LOTAD live in ponds and lakes, where they float on the surface. It grows weak if its broad leaf dies. On rare occasions, this POKéMON travels on land in search of clean water.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LOMBRE is nocturnal - it will get active after dusk. It is also a mischief-maker. When this POKéMON spots anglers, it tugs on their fishing lines from beneath the surface and enjoys their consternation.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LUDICOLO begins dancing as soon as it hears cheerful, festive music. This POKéMON is said to appear when it hears the singing of children on hiking outings.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SEEDOT attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. It sucks moisture from the tree while hanging off the branch. The more water it drinks, the glossier this POKéMON’s body becomes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: NUZLEAF live in densely overgrown forests. They occasionally venture out of the forest to startle people. This POKéMON dislikes having its long nose pinched.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SHIFTRY is a mysterious POKéMON that is said to live atop towering trees  dating back over a thousand years. It creates terrific windstorms with the fans it holds.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: TAILLOW courageously stands its ground against foes, however strong they may be. This gutsy POKéMON will remain defiant even after a loss. On the other hand, it cries loudly if it becomes hungry.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SWELLOW flies high above our heads, making graceful arcs in the sky. This POKéMON dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. The hapless prey is tightly grasped by SWELLOW’s clawed feet, preventing escape.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: WINGULL has the habit of carrying prey and valuables in its beak and hiding them in all sorts of locations. This POKéMON rides the winds and flies as if it were skating across the sky.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: PELIPPER is a flying transporter that carries small POKéMON and eggs inside its massive bill. This POKéMON builds its nest on steep cliffs facing the sea.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: RALTS senses the emotions of people using the horns on its head. This POKéMON rarely appears before people. But when it does, it draws closer if it senses that the person has a positive disposition.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is said that a KIRLIA that is exposed to the positive emotions of its TRAINER grows beautiful. This POKéMON controls psychokinetic powers with its highly developed brain.

sections.title: Psychic Devotion
content: Gardevoir has the ability to read the future. It is famously protective and will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole to protect its trainer.
It does not feel the pull of gravity because it supports its body with psychic energy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: From the tips of its feet, SURSKIT secretes an oil that enables it to walk on water as if it were skating. This POKéMON feeds on microscopic organisms in ponds and lakes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: MASQUERAIN intimidates enemies with the eyelike patterns on its antennas. This POKéMON flaps its four wings to freely fly in any direction - even sideways and backwards - as if it were a helicopter.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SHROOMISH live in damp soil in the dark depths of forests. They are often found keeping still under fallen leaves. This POKéMON feeds on compost that is made up of fallen, rotted leaves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: BRELOOM closes in on its foe with light and sprightly footwork, then throws punches with its stretchy arms. This POKéMON’s fighting technique puts boxers to shame.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SLAKOTH lolls around for over twenty hours every day. Because it moves so little, it does not need much food. This POKéMON’s sole daily meal consists of just three leaves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: VIGOROTH is always itching and agitated to go on a wild rampage. It simply can’t tolerate sitting still for even a minute. This POKéMON’s stress level rises if it can’t be moving constantly.

sections.title: Economic Energy Management
content: Slaking spends its entire day lounging and eating grass. It only moves when it has absolutely no other choice.
Despite its lazy appearance, it can exert horrifying power by releasing all its pent-up energy at once.

sections.title: Stat Anomaly
content: Slaking has a base stat total higher than many Legendary Pokémon, which is balanced by its 'Truant' ability that forces it to skip every other turn.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: NINCADA lives underground for many years in complete darkness. This POKéMON absorbs nutrients from the roots of trees. It stays motionless as it waits for evolution.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: NINJASK moves around at such a high speed that it cannot be seen, even while its crying can be clearly heard. For that reason, this POKéMON was long believed to be invisible.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SHEDINJA’s hard body doesn’t move - not even a twitch. In fact, its body appears to be merely a hollow shell. It is believed that this POKéMON will steal the spirit of anyone peering into its hollow body from its back.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Normally, WHISMUR’s voice is very quiet - it is barely audible even if one is paying close attention. However, if this POKéMON senses danger, it starts crying at an earsplitting volume.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LOUDRED’s bellowing can completely decimate a wood-frame house. It uses its voice to punish its foes. This POKéMON’s round ears serve as loudspeakers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: EXPLOUD triggers earthquakes with the tremors it creates by bellowing. If this POKéMON violently inhales from the ports on its body, it’s a sign that it is preparing to let loose a huge bellow.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: MAKUHITA is tenacious - it will keep getting up and attacking its foe however many times it is knocked down. Every time it gets back up, this POKéMON stores more energy in its body for evolving.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: HARIYAMA practices its straight-arm slaps in any number of locations. One hit of this POKéMON’s powerful, openhanded, straight-arm punches could snap a telephone pole in two.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: AZURILL spins its tail as if it were a lasso, then hurls it far. The momentum of the throw sends its body flying, too. Using this unique action, one of these POKéMON managed to hurl itself a record 33 feet.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: NOSEPASS’s magnetic nose is always pointed to the north. If two of these POKéMON meet, they cannot turn their faces to each other when they are close because their magnetic noses repel one another.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SKITTY has the habit of becoming fascinated by moving objects and chasing them around. This POKéMON is known to chase after its own tail and become dizzy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: DELCATTY prefers to live an unfettered existence in which it can do as it pleases at its own pace. Because this POKéMON eats and sleeps whenever it decides, its daily routines are completely random.

sections.title: Subterranean Scavenger
content: Sableye lives in caves where it leads a quiet life. It feeds on gemstones it finds in the earth, which has caused its eyes to transform into gems.
It is feared by many who believe it can steal the spirits of those it gazes upon in the dark.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The 'King of the Safe Swap' in the Great League. Its 'Return' (purified) coverage and Ghost/Dark typing make it extremely difficult to counter without a dedicated Fairy-type.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: MAWHILE’s huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed. Its docile-looking face serves to lull its foe into letting down its guard. When the foe least expects it, MAWHILE chomps it with its gaping jaws.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This POKéMON has a body of steel. To make its body, ARON feeds on iron ore that it digs from mountains. Occasionally, it causes major trouble by eating bridges and rails.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LAIRON tempers its steel body by drinking highly nutritious mineral springwater until it is bloated. This POKéMON makes its nest close to springs of delicious water.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: AGGRON claims an entire mountain as its own territory. It mercilessly beats up anything that violates its environment. This POKéMON vigilantly patrols its territory at all times.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: MEDITITE undertakes rigorous mental training deep in the mountains. However, whenever it meditates, this POKéMON always loses its concentration and focus. As a result, its training never ends.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is said that through meditation, MEDICHAM heightens energy inside its body and sharpens its sixth sense. This POKéMON hides its presence by merging itself with fields and mountains.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: ELECTRIKE stores electricity in its long body hair. This POKéMON stimulates its leg muscles with electric charges. These jolts of power give its legs explosive acceleration performance.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: MANECTRIC is constantly discharging electricity from its mane. The sparks sometimes ignite forest fires. When it enters a battle, this POKéMON creates thunderclouds.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: PLUSLE always acts as a cheerleader for its partners. Whenever a teammate puts out a good effort in battle, this POKéMON shorts out its body to create the crackling noises of sparks to show its joy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: MINUN is more concerned about cheering on its partners than its own safety. It shorts out the electricity in its body to create brilliant showers of sparks to cheer on its teammates.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With the arrival of night, VOLBEAT emits light from its tail. It communicates with others by adjusting the intensity and flashing of its light. This POKéMON is attracted by the sweet aroma of ILLUMISE.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: ILLUMISE attracts a swarm of VOLBEAT using a sweet fragrance. Once the VOLBEAT have gathered, this POKéMON leads the lit-up swarm in drawing geometric designs on the canvas of the night sky.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: ROSELIA shoots sharp thorns as projectiles at any opponent that tries to steal the flowers on its arms. The aroma of this POKéMON brings serenity to living things.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach comprises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When SWALOT spots prey, it spurts out a hideously toxic fluid from its pores and sprays the target. Once the prey has weakened, this POKéMON gulps it down whole with its cavernous mouth.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CARVANHA’s strongly developed jaws and its sharply pointed fangs pack the destructive power to rip out boat hulls. Many boats have been attacked and sunk by this POKéMON.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Nicknamed “the bully of the sea,” SHARPEDO is widely feared. Its cruel fangs grow back immediately if they snap off. Just one of these POKéMON can thoroughly tear apart a supertanker.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: WAILMER’s nostrils are located above its eyes. This playful POKéMON loves to startle people by forcefully snorting out seawater it stores inside its body out of its nostrils.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: WAILORD is the largest of all identified POKéMON up to now. This giant POKéMON swims languorously in the vast open sea, eating massive amounts of food at once with its enormous mouth.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: NUMEL is extremely dull witted - it doesn’t notice being hit. However, it can’t stand hunger for even a second. This POKéMON’s body is a seething cauldron of boiling magma.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CAMERUPT has a volcano inside its body. Magma of 18,000 degrees F courses through its body. Occasionally, the humps on this POKéMON’s back erupt, spewing the superheated magma.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: TORKOAL digs through mountains in search of coal. If it finds some, it fills hollow spaces on its shell with the coal and burns it. If it is attacked, this POKéMON spouts thick black smoke to beat a retreat.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SPOINK bounces around on its tail. The shock of its bouncing makes its heart pump. As a result, this POKéMON cannot afford to stop bouncing - if it stops, its heart will stop.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: GRUMPIG uses the black pearls on its body to amplify its psychic power waves for gaining total control over its foe. When this POKéMON uses its special power, its snorting breath grows labored.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: All the SPINDA that exist in the world are said to have utterly unique spot patterns. The shaky, tottering steps of this POKéMON give it the appearance of dancing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: TRAPINCH’s nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit dug in sand. This POKéMON patiently waits for prey to tumble down the pit. Its giant jaws have enough strength to crush even boulders.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To make prey faint, VIBRAVA generates ultrasonic waves by vigorously making its two wings vibrate. This POKéMON’s ultrasonic waves are so powerful, they can bring on headaches in people.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: FLYGON is nicknamed “the elemental  spirit of the desert.” Because its flapping wings whip up a cloud of sand, this POKéMON is always enveloped in a sandstorm while flying.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CACNEA lives in arid locations such as deserts. It releases a strong aroma from its flower to attract prey. When prey comes near, this POKéMON shoots sharp thorns from its body to bring the victim down.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: During the daytime, CACTURNE remains unmoving so that it does not lose any moisture to the harsh desert sun. This POKéMON becomes active at night when the temperature drops.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SWABLU has light and fluffy wings that are like cottony clouds. This POKéMON is not frightened of people. It lands on the heads of people and sits there like a cotton-fluff hat.

sections.title: Aerial Soprano
content: Altaria dances and wheels through the sky among billowy, cotton-like clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano voice that puts listeners into a dreamy state.
It is very affectionate; if it bonds with a person, it will envelop them with its soft, fluffy wings and hum.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A foundational pillar of the Great League meta. Its extreme bulk and Dragon Breath pressure allow it to win most neutral matchups comfortably.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has feuded with Seviper for many generations. Its sharp claws are its biggest weapons.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SEVIPER shares a generations-long feud with ZANGOOSE. The scars on its body are evidence of vicious battles. This POKéMON attacks using its sword- edged tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LUNATONE was discovered at a location where a meteorite fell. As a result, some people theorize that this POKéMON came from space. However, no one has been able to prove this theory so far.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SOLROCK is a new species of POKéMON that is said to have fallen from space. It floats in air and moves silently. In battle, this POKéMON releases intensely bright light.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: BARBOACH’s sensitive whiskers serve as a superb radar system. This POKéMON hides in mud, leaving only its two whiskers exposed while it waits for prey to come along.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: WHISCASH is extremely territorial. Just one of these POKéMON will claim a large pond as its exclusive territory. If a foe approaches it, it thrashes about and triggers a massive earthquake.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CORPHISH were originally foreign POKéMON that were imported as pets. They eventually turned up in the wild. This POKéMON is very hardy and has greatly increased its population.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CRAWDAUNT has an extremely violent nature that compels it to challenge other living things to battle. Other life-forms refuse to live in ponds inhabited by this POKéMON, making them desolate places.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: BALTOY moves while spinning around on its one foot. Primitive wall paintings depicting this POKéMON living among people were discovered in some ancient ruins.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CLAYDOL are said to be dolls of mud made by primitive humans and brought to life by exposure to a mysterious ray. This POKéMON moves about while levitating.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lived on the seafloor 100 million years ago and was reanimated scientifically.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CRADILY roams around the ocean floor in search of food. This POKéMON freely extends its tree trunk-like neck and captures unwary prey using its eight tentacles.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: ANORITH was regenerated from a prehistoric fossil. This primitive POKéMON once lived in warm seas. It grips its prey firmly between its two large claws.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: ARMALDO’s tough armor makes all attacks bounce off. This POKéMON’s two enormous claws can be freely extended or contracted. They have the power to punch right through a steel slab.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: FEEBAS’s fins are ragged and tattered from the start of its life. Because of its shoddy appearance, this POKéMON is largely ignored. It is capable of living in both the sea and in rivers.

sections.title: Aesthetic Pacifism
content: Milotic is said to be the most beautiful of all Pokémon. It has the power to becalm such emotions as anger and hostility to end arguments.
It lives at the bottom of large lakes. When its body glows a vivid pink, it releases a wave of energy that is soothing to the soul.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CASTFORM’s appearance changes with the weather. This POKéMON gained the ability to use the vast power of nature to protect its tiny body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: KECLEON is capable of changing its body colors at will to blend in with its surroundings. There is one exception - this POKéMON can’t change the zigzag pattern on its belly.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SHUPPET is attracted by feelings of jealousy and vindictiveness. If someone develops strong feelings of vengeance, this POKéMON will appear in a swarm and line up beneath the eaves of that person’s home.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A doll that became a Pokémon over its grudge from being junked. It seeks the child that disowned it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: DUSKULL can pass through any wall no matter how thick it may be. Once this POKéMON chooses a target, it will doggedly pursue the intended victim until the break of dawn.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: DUSCLOPS’s body is completely hollow - there is nothing at all inside. It is said that its body is like a black hole. This POKéMON will absorb anything into its body, but nothing will ever come back out.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives in tropical jungles. The bunch of fruit around its neck is delicious. The fruit grows twice a year.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CHIMECHO makes its cries echo inside its hollow body. When this POKéMON becomes enraged, its cries result in ultrasonic waves that have the power to knock foes flying.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Every time ABSOL appears before people, it is followed by a disaster such as an earthquake or a tidal wave. As a result, it came to be known as the disaster POKéMON.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: WYNAUT can always be seen with a big, happy smile on its face. Look at its tail to determine if it is angry. When angered, this POKéMON will be slapping the ground with its tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SNORUNT live in regions with heavy snowfall. In seasons without snow, such as spring and summer, this POKéMON steals away to live quietly among stalactites and stalagmites deep in caverns.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: GLALIE has a body made of rock, which it hardens with an armor of ice. This POKéMON has the ability to freeze moisture in the atmosphere into any shape it desires.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SPHEAL is much faster rolling than  walking to get around. When groups of this POKéMON eat, they all clap at once to show their pleasure. Because of this, their mealtimes are noisy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a very sensitive nose. It touches new things with its nose to examine them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: WALREIN’s two massively developed tusks can totally shatter blocks of ice weighing ten tons with one blow. This POKéMON’s thick coat of blubber insulates it from subzero temperatures.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: CLAMPERL’s sturdy shell is not only good for protection - it is also used for clamping and catching prey. A fully grown CLAMPERL’s shell will be scored with nicks and scratches all over.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: HUNTAIL’s presence went unnoticed by people for a long time because it lives at extreme depths in the sea. This POKéMON’s eyes can see clearly even in the murky dark depths of the ocean.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: GOREBYSS lives in the southern seas at extreme depths. Its body is built to withstand the enormous pressure of water at incredible depths. Because of this, this POKéMON’s body is unharmed by ordinary attacks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: RELICANTH is a POKéMON species that existed for a hundred million years without ever changing its form. This ancient POKéMON feeds on microscopic organisms with its toothless mouth.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LUVDISC live in shallow seas in the tropics. This heart-shaped POKéMON earned its name by swimming after loving couples it spotted in the ocean’s waves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: BAGON has a dream of one day soaring in the sky. In doomed efforts to fly, this POKéMON hurls itself off cliffs. As a result of its dives, its head has  grown tough and as hard as tempered steel.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Inside SHELGON’s armor-like shell, cells are in the midst of transformation to create an entirely new body. This POKéMON’s shell is extremely heavy, making its movements sluggish.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: SALAMENCE came about as a result of a strong, long-held dream of growing wings. It is said that this powerful desire triggered a sudden mutation in this POKéMON’s cells, causing it to sprout its magnificent wings.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When two BELDUM fuse together, METANG is formed. The brains of the BELDUM are joined by a magnetic nervous system. By linking its brains magnetically, this POKéMON generates strong psychokinetic power.

sections.title: Organic Supercomputer
content: Metagross has four brains that are joined by a complex neural network. As a result, it can perform difficult intellectual calculations faster than a supercomputer.
When it hunts, it pins its prey to the ground under its massive body. It then eats the victim with the mouth on its stomach.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The undisputed master of the Master League Premiere format. Its combination of Meteor Mash and high-defense Steel typing makes it a mandatory inclusion for many teams.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: REGIROCK was sealed away by people long ago. If this POKéMON’s body is damaged in battle, it is said to seek out suitable rocks on its own to repair itself.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: REGICE’s body was made during an ice age. The deep-frozen body can’t be melted, even by fire. This POKéMON controls frigid air of minus 328 degrees F.

sections.title: Extraterrestrial Metallurgy
content: Registeel's body is made of a strange material that is harder than any metal on Earth. It is hollow inside, and no one knows what it eats.
The metal that composes its body is tempered by pressure deep underground over tens of thousands of years.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Often cited as the most 'un-fun' Pokémon to face in GBL. Its astronomical bulk and high-damage Lock-On energy generation allow it to spam powerful moves like Zap Cannon and Focus Blast.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LATIAS is highly sensitive to the emotions of people. If it senses any hostility, this POKéMON ruffles the feathers all over its body and cries shrilly to intimidate the foe.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: LATIOS has the ability to make its foe see an image of what it has seen or imagines in its head. This POKéMON is intelligent and understands human speech.

sections.title: Abyssal Sovereign
content: Kyogre has the power to control water and create massive rain clouds. It has been referred to as a savior by people who have suffered from droughts.
It is said to have expanded the seas by covering the earth with torrential rains and towering tidal waves.

sections.title: Tectonic Titan
content: Groudon is said to be the personification of the land itself. It has the power to scatter rain clouds and make water evaporate with light and heat.
It has been locked in a traditional clash with Kyogre since ancient times as each sought to expand their respective domain.

sections.title: Ozone Arbiter
content: Rayquaza lived in the ozone layer for hundreds of millions of years without ever descending to the ground. It feeds on water and particles in the atmosphere.
It acts as a mediator in the conflict between Kyogre and Groudon, descending to Earth to quell their destructive battles.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A legend states that JIRACHI will make true any wish that is written on notes attached to its head when it awakens. If this POKéMON senses danger, it will fight without awakening.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The DNA of a space virus underwent a sudden mutation upon exposure to a laser beam and resulted in DEOXYS. The crystalline organ on this POKéMON’s chest appears to be its brain.

sections.title: Photosynthetic Shell
content: Turtwig's shell is made of soil and hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakeshores.
The leaf on its head will wilt if the Pokémon becomes thirsty. It carries out photosynthesis with its entire body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A GROTLE that lives in the forest is said to have its own secret springwater.

sections.title: Migratory Ecosystem
content: Torterra are so large and stationary that small Pokémon occasionally build nests on their backs.
Some people once believed that a giant Torterra lived under the earth and supported the continents on its back.

sections.title: Gas-Powered Tail
content: Chimchar's tail is fueled by gas produced in its stomach. Even rain can't put out the fire.
When it sleeps, the flame on its tail goes out to prevent forest fires.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To intimidate attackers, it stretches the fire on its tail to make itself appear bigger.

sections.title: Pyro-Kinetic Martial Arts
content: Infernape uses a unique type of martial arts involving all of its limbs. Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature.
It is incredibly agile, using its speed to confuse enemies while delivering powerful punches and kicks.

sections.title: Proud Navigator
content: Piplup is very proud and dislikes accepting food from people. It is a skilled swimmer and can dive for over 10 minutes.
Its thick down protects it from the freezing cold of the northern seas where it makes its home.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

sections.title: Ice-Slicing Wings
content: The three horns on its beak are a symbol of its strength. The leader of a colony has the largest horns.
