Its wings are sharp enough to slice through thick ice floes. It swims as fast as a jet boat.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A versatile Water/Steel type. Steel Wing's energy generation allows it to reach Hydro Cannon and Drill Peck quickly, making it a threat in both Great and Ultra Leagues.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a savage nature. It will courageously challenge foes that are much larger.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A comparison revealed that Bidoof’s front teeth grow at the same rate as Rattata’s.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It shakes its head back to front, causing its antennae to hit each other and sound like a xylophone.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It crosses its knifelike arms in front of its chest when it cries. It can compose melodies ad lib.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has eyes that can see through anything. It spots and captures prey hiding behind objects.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Over the winter, it closes its bud and endures the cold. In spring, the bud opens and releases pollen.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It attracts prey with a sweet aroma, then downs it with thorny whips hidden in its arms.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its skull is as hard as iron.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its powerful head butt has enough power to shatter even the most durable things upon impact.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A Pokémon that lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its facial hide is extremely hard.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Any frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by COMBEE.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A Pokémon formed by three others. It busily carries sweet floral honey to VESPIQUEN.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by COMBEE.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It blooms during times of strong sunlight. It tries to make up for everything it endured as a bud.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its colors and shapes differ from region to region. In the Sinnoh region, two types are confirmed.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a pliable body without any bones. If any part of its body is torn off, it grows right back.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s drowzy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: An extremely cautious Pokémon. It cloaks its body with its fluffy ear fur when it senses danger.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its cries sound like incantations. Those hearing it are tormented by headaches and hallucinations.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Becoming active at night, it is known to swarm with numerous MURKROW in tow.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon’s nest and claims it as its own.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It emits cries by agitating an orb at the back of its throat. It moves with flouncing hops.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It sprays a vile-smelling fluid from the tip of its tail to attack. Its range is over 160 feet.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: One caused a news sensation when it was dug up at a construction site after a 2,000-year sleep.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It looks as if it is always crying. It is actually adjusting its body’s fluid levels by eliminating excess.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: In an attempt to confuse its enemy, it mimics the enemy’s movements. Then it wastes no time in making itself scarce!

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It loves round white things. It carries an egg-shaped rock in imitation of CHANSEY.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

sections.title: Spectral Bindings
content: Spiritomb was formed by 108 spirits that were bound to an Odd Keystone 500 years ago as punishment for misdeeds.
It projects its gaseous form from the stone to attack, but it can never stray far from its anchor.

sections.title: Numerical Significance
content: Heavily influenced by the Buddhist tradition of 108 temptations. Its weight and Pokedex number also reflect this number in various game datasets.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It nests in small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: There is a long-held belief that medicine made from its scales will heal even incurable illnesses.

sections.title: Supersonic Predator
content: Garchomp can fly at speeds rivaling a jet plane. It never lets its prey escape. The scales on its body reduce air resistance.
It lives in volcanic caves and is known to be extremely territorial and aggressive when hunting.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A Master League titan. Mud Shot allows it to spam Sand Tomb for debuffs or Earth Power for massive damage. Its high attack stat makes it a premiere Ground-type attacker.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It wolfs down its weight in food once a day, swallowing food whole with almost no chewing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

sections.title: Aura Perception
content: Lucario can sense the auras of all things. It can read the thoughts and movements of its enemies by sensing their aura fluctuations.
It is said that a well-trained Lucario can identify the emotions of creatures over half a mile away.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives in arid places. Instead of perspiration, it expels grainy sand from its body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its cheeks hold poison sacs. It tries to catch foes off guard to jab them with toxic fingers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It attracts prey with its sweet- smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: After long exposure to sunlight, the patterns on its tail fins shine vividly when darkness arrives.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A friendly Pokémon that captures the subtle flows of seawater using its two antennae.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives on snowy mountains. Having had little contact with humans, it is boldly inquisitive.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It whips up blizzards in mountains that are always buried in snow. It is the abominable snowman.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They communicate by clawing signs in boulders and work together to surround enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. GEODUDE are shot at rare times.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees F from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It will never appear where there is strife. Its sightings have become rare recently.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: By churning its wings, it creates shock waves that inflict critical internal injuries to foes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Just like a plant, it uses photosynthesis. As a result, it is always enveloped in clear air.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: As a protective technique, it can completely freeze its fur to make its hairs stand like needles.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Additional software was installed to make it a better Pokémon. It began acting oddly, however.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It freely controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The antenna on its head captures radio waves from the world of spirits that command it to take people there.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It freezes foes with an icy breath nearly -60 degrees F. What seems to be its body is actually hollow.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf all came from the same egg.

sections.title: Chronological Sovereign
content: Dialga has the power to control time. It is said that time began moving when Dialga was born.
It is a legendary Pokémon of Sinnoh that is spoken of in ancient myths as a deity.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The definitive 'King of the Master League.' Its Steel/Dragon typing and the high-damage Dragon Breath fast move make it the central pillar around which the entire league meta revolves.

sections.title: Dimensional Sovereign
content: Palkia has the ability to distort space. It is said to live in a gap in the spatial dimension parallel to ours.
It is described in Sinnoh myths as a deity that maintains the stability of the physical world.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

sections.title: Distortion Dweller
content: Giratina was banished to the Distortion World for its violent behavior. It silently gazes at the old world from its home.
In its Origin Forme, it can travel between dimensions at will. It possesses terrifying spectral power.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Altered Forme is a premiere tank in the Ultra League, while Origin Forme dominates the Master League. Its Ghost/Dragon typing is both a blessing and a curse in high-level play.

sections.title: Nocturnal Serenity
content: Cresselia represents the crescent moon. Its feathers are used as charms to dispel nightmares and bring pleasant dreams.
It emits particles of light from its veil-like wings that create a soothing, aurora-like glow in the night sky.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: One of the bulkiest Pokémon in the Great and Ultra Leagues. Its ability to absorb massive amounts of neutral damage and retaliate with Grass Knot or Moonblast makes it a frustrating opponent to knock out.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A Pokémon that lives in warm seas. It inflates the flotation sac on its head to drift and search for food.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Born on a cold seafloor, it will swim great distances to return to its birthplace.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives in flower patches and avoids detection by curling up to look like a flowering plant.

sections.title: Primeval Architect
content: According to the legends of Sinnoh, Arceus emerged from an egg in a place where there was nothing and then shaped the entire world.
It possesses 1,000 arms with which it is said to have constructed the universe. It can change its type by holding different elemental plates.

sections.title: Infinite Energy Source
content: Victini creates an unlimited supply of energy within its body and shares it with those who touch it. When it shares this energy, the recipient's body overflows with power.
It is said that a Trainer with a Victini will always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

sections.title: Photosynthetic Elegance
content: Snivy is very intelligent and calm. It exposes its tail to sunlight to perform photosynthesis, which makes its movements much swifter.
If it is not feeling well, its tail will droop. It is known to be quite snobbish toward Trainers it deems unworthy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

sections.title: Intimidating Gaze
content: Serperior only gives its all against opponents that are not fazed by its noble, intimidating glare. It raises its head to look down on its foes.
It can amplify solar energy within its body to unleash powerful Grass-type attacks.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A bulky Grass-type specialist in the Great League. Its access to Frenzy Plant and Aerial Ace allows it to counter Fighters and other Grass-types effectively.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can deftly dodge its foe’s attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.

sections.title: Seamitar Mastery
content: Samurott can silence its enemies with a simple glare. It carries armor made of shells on its forelegs, which it can unsheathe and use like swords.
One swing of its seamitar can fell an opponent in the blink of an eye. It is a master of martial arts.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The addition of Hydro Cannon via Community Day made Samurott a legitimate threat. Megahorn provides unique coverage that surprises Grass and Psychic-type switch-ins.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can’t help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Munna always float in the air. People whose dreams are eaten by them forget what the dreams had been about.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Each follows its Trainer’s orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can return to its Trainer’s location regardless of the distance separating them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females’ flying abilities surpass those of the males.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they’ll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its internal fire burns at 2,500° F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

sections.title: Dermal Defense
content: Scrafty uses its loose skin like a shield to reduce the damage it takes. It kicks with enough force to smash through large boulders.
It is very protective of its family and territory. The bigger its crest, the more respect it commands within its group.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A Great League staple. Counter and Power-Up Punch allow it to snowball quickly, while Foul Play provides high-damage STAB coverage.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They use psychic power to control their arms, which are made of a special liquid. They can crush boulders psychically.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck’s horns.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The energy made in its cheeks’ electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The fate of the ships and crew that wander into Jellicent’s habitat: all sunken, all lost, all vanished.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

sections.title: Electro-Static Webbing
content: Galvantula creates webs made of electrically charged silk. When prey touches the web, they are paralyzed by a massive surge of electricity.
It can fire its electrified fur like needles to defend itself from attackers.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The definitive 'anti-Flyer' in the Great League. Volt Switch combined with Discharge and Lunge creates immense pressure, while Lunge's guaranteed Attack debuff increases its effective bulk.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent’s brain, causing unendurable headaches.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can manipulate an opponent’s memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they’ve been fighting.

sections.title: Unbreakable Tusks
content: Haxorus has tusks that are so sharp and strong they can cut through steel beams. It is generally kind but becomes terrifying if its territory is invaded.
Its body is covered in hard armor, and it uses its axe-like jaws to deliver finishing blows.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Blades comprise this Pokémon’s entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

sections.title: Tri-Headed Hostility
content: Hydreigon is extremely aggressive and will attack anything that moves. The heads on its arms do not have brains, but they are capable of eating.
It flies through the sky on its six wings, destroying everything in its path with its three mouths.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A high-damage Dragon in the Master League. Dragon Breath and Brutal Swing allow it to tear through neutral matchups, though its double weakness to Fairy remains a major liability.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun.

sections.title: Guardian of Justice
content: Cobalion has a body and heart of steel. It once protected Pokémon from humans by leading them to safety during a war.
Its glare is enough to make even the most unruly Pokémon obey it. It is the leader of the Swords of Justice.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The king of the Ultra League. Double Kick energy generation and Sacred Sword's efficiency make it a top-tier threat that almost every team must prepare for.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed.

sections.title: Incinerating Truth
content: Reshiram can scorch the entire world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.
When its tail flares up, the heat energy changes the atmosphere and causes weather changes across the globe.

sections.title: Aerodynamic Lightning
content: Zekrom can scorch the world with lightning. It assists those who want to build an ideal world.
Its tail contains a massive electricity generator that can produce enough power to darken the sky with thunderclouds.

sections.title: Agricultural Fertility
content: Landorus is known as the 'Guardian of the Fields.' The energy that pours from its tail provides nutrition to the soil, ensuring a bountiful harvest.
In its Therian Forme, it becomes much more aggressive and possesses overwhelming physical strength.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Therian Forme is a premiere threat in the Master League. The buff to Sandsear Storm has made it one of the most oppressive Ground-types in the format due to its guaranteed Attack debuff.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: By blasting water from its hooves, it can glide across water. It excels at using leg moves while battling.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Over 300 million years ago, it was feared as the strongest of hunters. It has been modified by Team Plasma.

sections.title: Armored Canopy
content: Chespin has a tough shell covering its head and back. Even if a truck crashed into it, Chespin would likely be unscathed.
It is naturally curious and often gets into trouble, but it can use its head spikes to protect itself from predators.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It relies on its sturdy shell to deflect predators’ attacks. It counterattacks with its sharp quills.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a twig stuck in its tail. With friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

sections.title: Pyromancy & Precognition
content: Delphox focuses its mind on the flame at the tip of its branch to see into the future. It uses psychic power to generate 5,400-degree-Fahrenheit fire.
By swirling its branch, it can create a massive vortex of fire that incinerates its foes instantly.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A niche Fire/Psychic pick. The addition of Blast Burn and Mystical Fire has given it significant utility, allowing it to debuff opponents while dealing high STAB damage.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

sections.title: Hydro-Ninja Mastery
content: Greninja creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can slice through metal.
It uses its long tongue like a scarf to mask its presence and keep its throat moist during rapid movement.

sections.title: Battle Bond Phenomenon
content: Famously associated with the Kalos region hero Ash Ketchum. Their unique 'Battle Bond' allowed Greninja to take on a special form that rivaled Mega Evolutions in raw power.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The definitive 'spam' king of the Ultra League. Water Shuriken's elite energy generation allows it to reach Hydro Cannon and Night Slash faster than almost any other Pokémon can react.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

sections.title: High-Speed Incineration
content: Talonflame dives at its prey at speeds of over 310 mph. It finishes them off with a powerful kick and showers of embers from its wings.
When it gets excited, it vents embers from the gaps between its feathers. It is extremely popular among aerial racers.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A top-tier threat in the Great and Ultra Leagues. Incinerate provides massive damage, while the Fly/Brave Bird/Flame Charge combination creates a high-stakes psychological game for the opponent.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When under attack from bird Pokémon, it spews a poisonous black powder that causes paralysis on contact.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it quickly bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Vivillon with many different patterns are found all over the world. These patterns are affected by the climate of their habitat.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It draws out and controls the hidden power of flowers. The flower Flabébé holds is most likely part of its body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It flutters around fields of flowers and cares for flowers that are starting to wilt. It draws out the hidden power of flowers to battle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It claims exquisite flower gardens as its territory, and it obtains power from basking in the energy emitted by flowering plants.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can tell how its Trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathic sense lets them run as if one being.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: From the slight twitches of its bamboo leaf, it deduces its opponent’s movements. It’s eager to tussle but kindhearted toward its companions.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Apparently this Pokémon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords, which cooperate via telepathy to coordinate attacks and slash their enemies to ribbons.

sections.title: Spectral Monarchy
content: Aegislash has the power to detect the innate qualities of a leader. Legend says that whoever Aegislash chooses will soon become a king.
It can manipulate people and Pokémon with its spectral power, using them to do its bidding.

sections.title: Stance Change Mechanics
content: In the main series games, its 'Stance Change' ability allows it to flip its Attack and Defense stats depending on the move used, a mechanic that translates into high-difficulty gameplay.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It emits a scent that enraptures those who smell it. This fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It devises various scents, pleasant and unpleasant, and emits scents that its enemies dislike in order to gain an edge in battle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and sticky as cotton candy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When they evolve, two Binacle multiply into seven. They fight with the power of seven Binacle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Camouflaged as rotten kelp, they spray liquid poison on prey that approaches unawares and then finish it off.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Their enormous claws launch cannonballs of water powerful enough to pierce tanker hulls.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn’t like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The diamond-shaped crystals on its body expel air as cold as -240 degrees Fahrenheit, surrounding its enemies and encasing them in ice.

sections.title: Ribbon-Like Feelers
content: Sylveon sends a soothing aura from its ribbon-like feelers to calm fights. It will wrap these feelers around its Trainer's arm as it walks.
Despite its gentle appearance, it is a ferocious fighter and can use its ribbons to distract and bind its prey.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The 'Charmer' of choice for the Ultra and Master Leagues. Its high bulk and the high-damage Charm fast move allow it to shred Fighting, Dragon, and Dark types with ease.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Born from the temperatures and pressures deep underground, it fires beams from the stone in its head.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn’t dry out.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These Pokémon are created when spirits possess rotten tree stumps. They prefer to live in abandoned forests.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can control trees at will. It will trap people who harm the forest, so they can never leave.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It blocks opponents’ attacks with the ice that shields its body. It uses cold air to repair any cracks with new ice.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its ice-covered body is as hard as steel. Its cumbersome frame crushes anything that stands in its way.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They live in pitch-black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. Nothing can beat them in a battle in the dark.

sections.title: Eternal Vitality
content: Xerneas is said to share eternal life. It took the form of a tree and slept for a thousand years when its life energy was depleted.
When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing its life force with all living things in the vicinity.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A Master League titan. Geomancy's elite energy generation allowed it to finally utilize its powerful charged moves, making it the premier Fairy-type pick in the highest tiers of GBL.

sections.title: Cocoon of Oblivion
content: Yveltal has the power to absorb the life energy of all living things. When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life force of everything around it before turning into a cocoon.
Its wings and tail feathers spread wide as it absorbs life energy, glowing a dark, ominous red.

sections.title: Ecosystem Guardian
content: Zygarde monitors the ecosystem from its deep cave. It is said that when the ecosystem falls into disarray, Zygarde appears and reveals its true power.
In its Complete Forme, it possesses power that exceeds that of Xerneas and Yveltal combined.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The undisputed 'Final Boss' of the Master League. Its Complete Forme has the highest stat product in GBL history, making it almost impossible to knock out without a dedicated counter.

sections.title: Crystalline Mutation
content: Diancie is a sudden mutation of Carbink. Its pink, glittering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.
It can instantly create diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This troublemaker sends anything and everything to faraway places using its loop, which can warp space.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It’s said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

sections.title: Nocturnal Sharpshooter
content: Rowlet is nocturnal and can see as clearly in the dark as in the day. It flies silently, using its feathers like sharp daggers.
It uses photosynthesis to store energy during the day while it sleeps. Its head can rotate nearly 180 degrees to keep watch.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.

sections.title: Spectral Marksmanship
content: Decidueye can fire an arrow-shaped quill in a tenth of a second with pinpoint accuracy. It is so stealthy that its presence is felt only after it has struck.
It hides its presence by nocking its quills into its wing strings and letting them loose from the shadows.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A versatile Grass/Ghost type. Its access to Spirit Shackle allows it to trap opponents, while Frenzy Plant provides reliable high-damage output in the Great League.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire.

sections.title: Pyro-Mechanical Combat
content: Incineroar's fighting spirit increases when the crowd gets excited. It can launch fireballs from its belt-like pouch.
It is a master of rough-and-tumble combat, often disregarding its own safety to land a powerful blow on its opponent.

sections.title: The Heel Wrestler
content: Based on the 'Heel' (antagonist) in professional wrestling. Its design combines the features of a tiger with the persona of a flamboyant brawler.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It’s famous for being a hard worker.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A skillful dancer, it creates a sequence of water balloons as it dances, and briskly bombards its enemies.

sections.title: Hydro-Sonic Aria
content: Primarina uses songs to control balloons made of water. It manipulates these balloons with the sound waves from its voice.
Its singing is so beautiful that it is often used in theatrical performances. In battle, it uses its voice to unleash powerful elemental attacks.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A top-tier threat in the Master League. Charming down Dragons and Dark-types while reaching high-damage Hydro Cannons makes it a core meta pick.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It can peck at a rate of 16 times a second to drill holes in trees. It uses the holes for food storage and for nesting.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It eats berries and stores their seeds in its beak. When it encounters enemies or prey, it fires off all the seeds in a burst.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it battles, its beak heats up. The temperature can easily exceed 212 degrees Fahrenheit, causing severe burns when it hits.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With its sharp fangs, it will bite anything. It did not originally live in Alola but was imported from another region.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it finds a trace of its prey, it patiently stakes out the location...but it’s always snoozing by nightfall.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its strong jaw enables it to scrape trees and slurp out the sap. It normally lives underground.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its body is capable of storing electricity. On camping trips, people are grateful to have one around.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It zips around, on sharp lookout for an opening. It concentrates electrical energy within its large jaws and uses it to zap its enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: While guarding its weak points with its pincers, it looks for an opening and unleashes punches. When it loses, it foams at the mouth and faints.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It aimed for the top but got lost and ended up on a snowy mountain. Being forced to endure the cold, this Pokémon evolved and grew fur.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It beats its wings together to create fire. As it moves in the steps of its beautiful dance, it bathes opponents in intense flames.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It feeds on the nectar and pollen of flowers. Because it’s able to sense auras, it can identify which flowers are about to bloom.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s considered to be a good Pokémon for beginners because of its friendliness, but its disposition grows rougher as it grows up.

sections.title: Lunar Temperament
content: Lycanroc has three distinct forms depending on the time of day it evolved. Midnight Forme is highly aggressive and enjoys taunting its foes.
It is extremely loyal to its Trainer, but its behavioral shift during the full moon can make it difficult to control.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it’s in trouble, its eyes moisten and begin to shine. The shining light attracts its comrades, and they stand together against their enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Toxapex crawls along the ocean floor on its 12 legs. It leaves a trail of Corsola bits scattered in its wake.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The mud stuck to Mudbray’s hooves enhances its grip and its powerful running gait.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It spits a mud that provides resistance to both wind and rain, so the walls of old houses were often coated with it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: During the day, it sleeps and soaks up light. When night falls, it walks around looking for a safer place to sleep.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It requires a lot of effort to maintain Lurantis’s vivid coloring, but some collectors enjoy this work and treat it as their hobby.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Forests where Shiinotic live are treacherous to enter at night. People confused by its strange lights can never find their way home again.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It burns its bodily fluids to create a poisonous gas. When its enemies become disoriented from inhaling the gas, it attacks them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit that it lives with.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A delectable aroma pours from its body. They are often swallowed whole by Toucannon lured by that wafting deliciousness.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The sepals on its head developed to protect its body. These are quite hard, so even if pecked by bird Pokémon, this Pokémon is totally fine.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its long, striking legs aren’t just for show but to be used to kick with skill. In victory, it shows off by kicking the defeated, laughing boisterously.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It attaches flowers to its highly nutritious vine. This revitalizes the flowers, and they give off an aromatic scent.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Known for its extreme intelligence, this Pokémon will look down on inexperienced Trainers, so it’s best suited to veteran Trainers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They form groups of roughly 20 individuals. Their mutual bond is remarkable—they will never let down a comrade.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With a flashing slash of its giant sharp claws, it cleaves seawater—or even air—right in two.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Born from a sand mound playfully built by a child, this Pokémon embodies the grudges of the departed.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Possessed people controlled by this Pokémon transformed its sand mound into a castle. As it evolved, its power to curse grew ever stronger.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It lives in shallow seas, such as areas near a beach. It can eject its internal organs, which it uses to engulf its prey or battle enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The heavy control mask it wears suppresses its intrinsic capabilities. This Pokémon has some hidden special power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its trust in its partner is what awakens it. This Pokémon is capable of changing its type, a flexibility that is well displayed in battle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Originally making its home in the ozone layer, it hurtles to the ground when the shell enclosing its body grows too heavy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is born asleep, and it dies asleep. All its movements are apparently no more than the results of it tossing and turning in its dreams.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The shell on its back is chemically unstable and explodes violently if struck. The hole in its stomach is its weak point.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The spiny fur on its back is normally at rest. When this Pokémon becomes agitated, its fur stands on end and stabs into its attackers.

sections.title: Spectral Loneliness
content: Mimikyu wears a rag that looks like Pikachu so that it can get closer to people and other Pokémon. It believes that its true form is too terrifying to be seen.
If its rag is damaged, it will spend all night tirelessly repairing it. Looking under its rag is said to cause a mysterious illness.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it unleashes its psychic power from the protuberance on its head, the grating sound of grinding teeth echoes through the area.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a compassionate personality, but if it is angered, it completely destroys its surroundings with its intense breath.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Swinging its massive anchor, it can KO Wailord in a single blow. What appears to be green seaweed is actually its body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o lives.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It leaps at its prey with a courageous shout. Its scaly punches tear its opponents to shreds.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it spots enemies, it threatens them by jingling the scales on its tail. Weak opponents will crack and flee in panic.

sections.title: Electric Guardian
content: Tapu Koko is the guardian deity of Melemele Island. It is known for its fickle nature and its tendency to suddenly attack those who trespass on its domain.
It can store electricity in its shells and unleash it in massive bolts to strike down its enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This guardian deity of Akala is guilelessly cruel. The fragrant aroma of flowers is the source of its energy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It pulls large trees up by the roots and swings them around. It causes vegetation to grow, and then it absorbs energy from the growth.

sections.title: Purification & Mists
content: Tapu Fini is the guardian deity of Poni Island. It has the power to create a thick mist that confuses and traps its enemies.
It is said to be able to purify water and heal those who are worthy of its favor.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The 'Queen of the Ultra League.' Its Water/Fairy typing and high bulk allow it to wall many of the league's most common threats, while Surf provides consistent damage.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Motionless as if dead, its body is faintly warm to the touch. In the distant past, it was called the cocoon of the stars.

sections.title: The Beast that Devours the Sun
content: Solgaleo is an emissary of the sun. It is said to live in another world and returns to ours by opening Ultra Wormholes.
Its body is filled with massive amounts of energy, and it shines brightly even in the middle of the night.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A massive tank in the Master League. Its Steel/Psychic typing provides numerous resistances, making it an excellent anchor for teams with high-damage glass cannons.

sections.title: The Beast that Calls the Moon
content: Lunala is an emissary of the moon. It is said to live in another world and returns to ours by opening Ultra Wormholes.
It absorbs light and converts it into energy. When it spreads its wings to absorb light and glitters, it resembles a beautiful night sky.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: One of several mysterious Ultra Beasts. People on the street report observing those infested by it suddenly becoming violent.

sections.title: Extra-Dimensional Musculature
content: Buzzwole is an Ultra Beast that emerged from an Ultra Wormhole. It is known for its extreme physical strength and its tendency to constantly show off its muscles.
It possesses a specialized organ that allows it to absorb the energy of its enemies to further enhance its own power.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A premier Fighter in the Great and Ultra Leagues. Counter combined with Lunge and Power-Up Punch allows it to debuff enemies while simultaneously boosting its own damage.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: One of the dangerous Ultra Beasts, it has been spotted running across the land at terrific speeds.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: One of the mysterious life-forms known as Ultra Beasts. Astonishing electric shocks emanate from its entire body, according to witnesses.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It appeared from the Ultra Wormhole. Witnesses observed it flying across the sky at high speed.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Ultra Beast came from the Ultra Wormhole. It seems not to attack enemies on its own, but its sharp body is a dangerous weapon in itself.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has gobbled mountains and swallowed whole buildings, according to reports. It’s one of the Ultra Beasts.

sections.title: Light-Starved Remnant
content: Necrozma is a mysterious life-form that is said to have come from another world. It is constantly seeking light and will violently attack anything that possesses it.
It can fuse with Solgaleo or Lunala to take on even more powerful forms, allowing it to regain some of its lost power.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Dusk Mane and Dawn Wings forms have redefined the Master League. Sunsteel Strike and Moongeist Beam are among the most efficient moves in the game, forcing a shift in the entire meta.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This artificial Pokémon, constructed more than 500 years ago, can understand human speech but cannot itself speak.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Able to conceal itself in shadows, it never appears before humans, so its very existence was the stuff of myth.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body. It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It appeared from an Ultra Wormhole. Each one appears to be made up of many life-forms stacked one on top of each other.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It’s apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack, they’ll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.

sections.title: Liquid Metal Prowess
content: Melmetal was once worshipped as a deity that could create metal. Its body is composed of liquid metal that can harden into a material stronger than steel.
It uses its heavy hex-nut arms to deliver devastating punches that can crush boulders into dust.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A classic Master League veteran. Its high bulk and access to the extremely fast Double Iron Bash make it one of the best shield-burners and safe-swaps in the format.

sections.title: Percussive Vitality
content: Grookey can drum with its stick to revitalize plants and flowers. The stick it uses was originally a branch from the forest where its troop lived.
As it beats its stick, it releases energy that can heal withered grass and cause buds to bloom.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

sections.title: Forest Rhythm
content: Rillaboom carries a stump drum that it can use to control the roots of trees. It is a calm leader that values the harmony of its group.
In battle, it uses its powerful drumming to unleash seismic waves that can knock opponents off balance.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A heavy-hitting Grass type in the Great and Ultra Leagues. Its access to Frenzy Plant and High Horsepower gives it excellent coverage against common Steel and Poison-type counters.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon’s body. Once that happens, it’s ready to fight at full power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its thick and fluffy fur protects it from the cold and enables it to use hotter fire moves.

sections.title: Pyrotechnic Footwork
content: Cinderace can juggle a pebble with its feet to transform it into a burning soccer ball. It has incredible leg strength and can outrun most opponents.
It uses its dazzling footwork to confuse foes, and its fireballs are so hot they can leave third-degree burns on contact.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won’t be able to resist weeping.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

sections.title: Hydro-Sniper Precision
content: Inteleon has various secret capabilities, such as a sharp knife hidden in its tail and the ability to shoot water from its fingertips at Mach 3.
It is highly intelligent and can calculate the trajectory of its shots with surgical precision, rarely missing a target.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn’t notice the loss.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn’t.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It barely moves, but it’s still alive. Hiding in its shell without food or water seems to have awakened its psychic powers.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The seeds attached to its cotton fluff are full of nutrients. It spreads them on the wind so that plants and other Pokémon can benefit from them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
