content: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

sections.title: Springy Resilience
content: Dubwool has a coat of wool that is so springy it can bounce off the ground like a rubber ball. Its horns are mainly for attracting mates.
Despite its soft appearance, it is very sturdy and can withstand powerful physical attacks by simply bouncing away from the impact.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The 'Spam King' of the Great League. Double Kick energy generation paired with Body Slam and Wild Charge makes it one of the most versatile and annoying safe-swaps in the meta.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Apparently the itch of its teething impels it to snap its jaws at anything in front of it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it’s just an apple and nothing more.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: As it digs, it swallows sand and stores it in its neck pouch. The pouch can hold more than 17 pounds of sand.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it contracts its body, over 220 pounds of sand sprays from its nose. If it ever runs out of sand, it becomes disheartened.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It’s very proud of that jaw.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon has a jaw that’s as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda’s flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When this Pokémon sounds as if it’s strumming a guitar, it’s actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures’ emotions. If you don’t have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it’s trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

sections.title: Defensive Taunting
content: Obstagoon has an incredibly loud voice that it uses to taunt opponents into attacking first. It then uses its thick fur to block and counter those attacks.
It rarely initiates an attack, preferring to wait for the opponent to lose their cool and leave an opening.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A premiere choice in the Great and Ultra Leagues. Counter and Night Slash allow it to boost its own attack while dealing high neutral damage, and Obstruct provides a unique tactical debuff.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Only Farfetch’d that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon’s leek withers, it will retire from combat.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it’s decorated with cream.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass’s orders are absolute.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Icy scales fall from its wings like snow as it flies over fields and mountains. The temperature of its wings is less than −290 degrees Fahrenheit.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun’s descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It’s also generating electricity.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It’s also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Though it’s able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its body resembles polished metal, and it’s both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It’s capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it isn’t battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

sections.title: The Fairy King's Sword
content: Zacian is known as the hero that saved the Galar region. It can cut through anything with its sword, which is said to be able to slice the very fabric of space.
In its Hero of Many Battles form, it is a pure Fairy type, but it gains the Steel type when it holds its rusted sword.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The undisputed 'Goddess' of the Master League. Its pure Fairy typing (Hero form) and incredible movepool including Quick Attack, Close Combat, and Wild Charge make it the most versatile threat in the format.

sections.title: The Shield that Guards the Realm
content: Zamazenta is the partner hero to Zacian. It uses its shield-like mane to deflect even the most powerful attacks without taking a scratch.
It is so heavy and sturdy that it can bring down a mountain by simply charging into it.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A massive force in the Master League. Its access to Snarl and Close Combat allows it to exert huge shield pressure, while its high defense makes it a difficult target to take down.

sections.title: The Source of Dynamax
content: Eternatus came to Earth inside a meteor 20,000 years ago. It absorbs energy from the land to stay active, which is the origin of the Dynamax phenomenon.
When it releases its full power, it takes on the terrifying Eternamax form, which is too large to even be measured by standard devices.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Kubfu trains hard to perfect its moves. The moves it masters will determine which form it takes when it evolves.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in the one-hit KO. Its strategy is to leap in close to foes and land a devastating blow with a hardened fist.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon is a cluster of electrical energy. It’s said that removing the rings on Regieleki’s body will unleash the Pokémon’s latent power.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: An academic theory proposes that Regidrago’s arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It’s also a belligerent Pokémon—anything it wants, it takes by force.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn’t use its sense of sight. Spectrier’s kicks are said to separate soul from body.

sections.title: The King of Bountiful Harvests
content: Calyrex was once a merciful king that ruled over the Galar region. It has the power to see all past, present, and future events.
By mounting its loyal steed (Glastrier or Spectrier), it can regain its true power and bring about a bountiful harvest across the land.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.

sections.title: Mire-Dwelling Colossus
content: Ursaluna is a massive Pokémon that evolved through the influence of the swampy terrain of the Hisui region. It has the power to manipulate peat and soil.
It is a highly skilled tracker, using its powerful sense of smell to find objects buried deep underground. Its physical strength is unmatched among most forest-dwellers.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: Ursaluna serves as a high-bulk Ground/Normal type. While its standard form is a Master League niche, its Bloodmoon variant is feared for its overwhelming special attack pressure.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname ”sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

sections.title: Aromatic Grooming
content: Sprigatito frequently grooms its fur to keep it clean and to enhance the sweet scent it emits. This scent has a calming effect on those around it.
It can perform photosynthesis by absorbing sunlight through its fur. It is known to be quite temperamental and easily gets lonely.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

sections.title: Floral Misdirection
content: Meowscarada uses its flower as a tool for misdirection. It can create illusions and perform incredible feats of magic to confuse its enemies.
It is a master of stealth, moving through the shadows with silent grace. Its signature move, Flower Trick, never misses its mark.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The combination of Crocalor’s fire energy and overflowing vitality has caused an egg-shaped fireball to appear on the Pokémon’s head.

sections.title: Fiery Serenade
content: Skeledirge has a small, bird-like spirit made of fire that sits on its snout. The bird spirit changes shape when Skeledirge sings its powerful songs.
Its singing is said to be so soul-stirring that it can soothe even the most savage of beasts. In battle, its voice unleashes waves of intense heat.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A foundational pillar of the Great and Ultra League meta. Incinerate paired with Disarming Voice and Shadow Ball makes it a versatile and extremely tanky threat.

sections.title: Gelatinous Grooming
content: Quaxly is very particular about its appearance. Its feathers secrete a specialized gel that repels water and dirt, keeping it looking pristine.
It has incredibly strong legs and is a skilled swimmer. It is naturally tidy and becomes stressed if its environment is messy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.

sections.title: Rhythmic Combat
content: Quaquaval uses its rhythmic dancing to confuse and overwhelm its enemies. Each of its movements is calculated to deliver a powerful strike.
Its feathers are as sharp as blades, and it can use them to slice through water and solid objects with ease.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon’s natural enemy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it’s in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Exhibiting great teamwork, they use their incisors to cut pieces out of any material that might be useful for a nest, then make off with them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The two little ones just appeared one day. The group might be a family of related Pokémon, but nobody knows for sure.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These Pokémon prefer to live in cities. They form flocks based on the color of their feathers, and they fight over territory.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey’s body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff’s face.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon can store energy in its large dewlap. Mabosstiff unleashes this energy all at once to blow away enemies.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: A soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can’t remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It gathers things up in an apron made from shed feathers added to the Pokémon’s chest feathers, then drops those things from high places for fun.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon changes its appearance if it hears its allies calling for help. Palafin will never show anybody its moment of transformation.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders—now numbering eight—to generate energy.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When this Pokémon detects danger, it will open up its crystalline petals and fire beams from its conical body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon wanders around snowy, icy areas. It protects its body with powerful muscles and a thick layer of fat under its skin.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

sections.title: Transcendental Fury
content: Annihilape was born when Primeape's rage reached a point beyond the physical limit. Its physical body could no longer contain its anger, allowing it to transcend into a spectral form.
It is perpetually enraged, and its spectral power increases with every blow it receives. It is feared for its relentless and savage fighting style.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: The definitive 'Fighter' of the modern Great and Ultra League eras. Counter paired with Night Slash and Shadow Ball provides near-perfect coverage against almost every meta threat.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It’s a risky move that puts everything on the line.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Now that the brain waves from the head and tail are synced up, the psychic power of this Pokémon is 10 times stronger than Girafarig’s.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It slightly resembles a Magneton that lived for 10,000 years and was featured in an article in a paranormal magazine.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon closely resembles a scientific weapon that a paranormal magazine claimed was sent to this planet by aliens.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Its shape is similar to a robot featured in a paranormal magazine article. The robot was said to have been created by an ancient civilization.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is very similar to a cyborg covered exclusively by a paranormal magazine. The cyborg was said to be the modified form of a certain athlete.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It resembles a certain Pokémon introduced in a paranormal magazine, described as the offspring of a Hydreigon that fell in love with a robot.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon resembles an unknown object described in a paranormal magazine as a UFO sent to observe humanity.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has some similarities to a Pokémon introduced in a dubious magazine as a Tyranitar from one billion years into the future.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.

sections.title: Hoarding Instincts
content: Gimmighoul lives inside a chest filled with old coins. It uses its spectral power to manipulate those who approach, forcing them to collect more coins for its hoard.
In its Roaming Form, it is much more elusive and is often seen carrying a single gold coin across the Paldea region.

sections.title: Solid Gold Physiology
content: Gholdengo's body is composed entirely of 1,000 gold coins. It is said to be exceptionally friendly and enjoys the company of others.
Its solid gold body provides it with incredible durability and resistance to many different types of elemental attacks.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A premiere Steel/Ghost type in the Master League. Its Good as Gold ability in the main series translates to a high resistance profile in GO, making it an excellent counter to Fairy and Psychic types.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The grudge of a person punished for writing the king’s evil deeds upon wooden tablets has clad itself in dead leaves to become a Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon can control 100 tons of fallen snow. It plays around innocently by leaping in and out of avalanches it has caused.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The fear poured into an ancient ritual vessel has clad itself in rocks and dirt to become a Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It has some similarities to a mad scientist’s invention covered in a paranormal magazine.

sections.title: Primeval Winged King
content: Koraidon is a mysterious Pokémon that is said to have come from the ancient past. It possesses overwhelming physical power and is capable of running at incredible speeds.
It can change its form to better suit its environment, allowing it to swim, climb, and fly with ease.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A devastating force in the Master League. Breaking Swipe's guaranteed Attack debuff and its high-damage Fighting/Dragon STAB moves make it a terrifying opponent for any team.

sections.title: Iron Serpent of the Future
content: Miraidon is a mysterious Pokémon that is said to have come from the distant future. Its body is composed of advanced mechanical parts that are powered by electricity.
It is capable of performing incredible feats of speed and agility, and its electrical attacks are powerful enough to overload standard power grids.

sections.title: GBL Tactical Analysis
content: A high-speed Electric/Dragon pick in the Master League. Its ability to outpace opponents and deliver massive neutral damage makes it a premiere choice for fast-paced aggressive teams.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It's named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old expedition journal.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Many of its physical characteristics match those of a Virizion from the future that was covered in a paranormal magazine.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Dipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Supposedly, the regrets of a tea ceremony master who died before perfecting his craft lingered in some matcha and became a Pokémon.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It pretends to be tea, trying to fool people into drinking it so it can drain their life-force. Its ruse is generally unsuccessful.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: After all its muscles were stimulated by the toxic chain around its neck, Okidogi transformed and gained a powerful physique.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: The chain is made from toxins that enhance capabilities. It stimulated Munkidori's brain and caused the Pokémon's psychic powers to bloom.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Fezandipiti owes its beautiful looks and lovely voice to the toxic stimulants emanating from the chain wrapped around its body.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: There are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging and spouting pillars of flame.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It's said to incinerate everything around it with lightning launched from its fur. Very little is known about this creature.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It resembles a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a Terrakion that had been modified by an evil organization.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: It resembles a mysterious object introduced in a paranormal magazine as a cutting-edge weapon shaped like a Cobalion.

sections.title: Biological Profile
content: Terapagos protects itself using its power to transform energy into hard crystals. This Pokémon is the source of the Terastal phenomenon.

sections.title: Toxic Subjugation
content: Pecharunt uses its toxic chains to subjugate others, forcing them to do its bidding. It is a highly intelligent and manipulative Pokémon.
It is said to have been responsible for many of the legends and conflicts that have occurred throughout the history of the Paldea region.

sections.title: Mythological Baseline
content: Inspired by Momotarō of Japanese folklore, specifically the 'Peach Boy' who led a group of animal companions to fight off an ogre.
